window_summary: This window tracks the recent coder/code-server release stream from v4.107.0-rc.1 through v4.122.1, capturing updates to underlying Code versions, new flags like --cookie-suffix and --reconnection-grace-time, trusted origin wildcard support, and fixes for native modules and app name propagation.

chunks.title: v4.107.0-rc.1
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: coder/code-server
Release: v4.107.0-rc.1
Tag: v4.107.0-rc.1; draft: False; prerelease: True; published_at: 2025-12-12T05:04:54Z; created_at: 2025-12-12T01:33:07Z
Release notes:
Code v1.107.0
### Changed
- Update to Code 1.107.0
### Added
- New `--cookie-suffix` flag that can be used to add a suffix to the cookie when
using the built-in password authentication. You can use this to avoid
collisions with other instances of code-server.
- Support a new property `authorizationHeaderToken` on the extension gallery
configuration. This will be added to marketplace requests as a bearer token
using the `Authorization` header.
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the coder/code-server release v4.107.0-rc.1, the project updates to Code 1.107.0. This release adds a new --cookie-suffix flag to append a suffix to the cookie when using built-in password authentication, helping avoid collisions with other code-server instances. It also supports a new authorizationHeaderToken property on the extension gallery configuration, which is sent as a bearer token in the Authorization header for marketplace requests.

chunks.title: v4.113.0-rc.1
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: coder/code-server
Release: v4.113.0-rc.1
Tag: v4.113.0-rc.1; draft: False; prerelease: True; published_at: 2026-03-26T20:28:28Z; created_at: 2026-03-26T20:04:11Z
Release notes:
Code v1.113.0
### Changed
- Update to Code 1.113.0.
- Trusted origins can now begin with `*.`. For example, `--trusted-origins
'*.coder.com'` will match both `coder.com` exactly and any domain that ends in
`.coder.com`.
### Added
- Add the `--reconnection-grace-time` flag and pass it through to VS Code. This
configures how many seconds clients have to reconnect before the session is
permanently killed and a new connection must be made by reloading the
window. The default is 10800 (three hours).
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the coder/code-server release v4.113.0-rc.1, the project updates to Code 1.113.0. Trusted origins can now begin with '*.'; for example, '--trusted-origins *.coder.com' matches both 'coder.com' exactly and any subdomain ending in '.coder.com'. A new --reconnection-grace-time flag is added and passed to VS Code, configuring how many seconds clients have to reconnect before the session is permanently killed; the default is 10800 seconds (three hours).

chunks.title: v4.113.0-rc.3
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: coder/code-server
Release: v4.113.0-rc.3
Tag: v4.113.0-rc.3; draft: False; prerelease: True; published_at: 2026-03-31T17:48:55Z; created_at: 2026-03-31T17:07:39Z
Release notes:
Code v1.113.0
### Changed
- Update to Code 1.113.0.
- Trusted origins can now begin with `*.`. For example, `--trusted-origins
'*.coder.com'` will match both `coder.com` exactly and any domain that ends in
`.coder.com`.
### Added
- Add the `--reconnection-grace-time` flag and pass it through to VS Code. This
configures how many seconds clients have to reconnect before the session is
permanently killed and a new connection must be made by reloading the
window. The default is 10800 (three hours).
Hopefully the last RC, please pardon the dust while we work out the kinks in the new build process.
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the coder/code-server release v4.113.0-rc.3, the project updates to Code 1.113.0. Trusted origins can now begin with '*.'; for example, '--trusted-origins *.coder.com' matches both 'coder.com' exactly and any subdomain ending in '.coder.com'. A new --reconnection-grace-time flag is added and passed to VS Code, configuring how many seconds clients have to reconnect before the session is permanently killed; the default is 10800 seconds (three hours). This release note also mentions it is hopefully the last RC while working out kinks in the new build process.

chunks.title: v4.113.1
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: coder/code-server
Release: v4.113.1
Tag: v4.113.1; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2026-04-03T20:13:04Z; created_at: 2026-04-03T19:32:54Z
Release notes:
Code v1.113.0
This is a re-release of v4.113.0 but with the correct Node binaries for arm64
and armv7l. Previously they were packaging the amd64 Node binary due to a
mistake while refactoring CI to use more of the upstream build scripts.
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the coder/code-server release v4.113.1, the project re-releases v4.113.0 with the correct Node binaries for arm64 and armv7l. Previously, the amd64 Node binary was packaged due to a mistake while refactoring CI to use more of the upstream build scripts.

chunks.title: v4.122.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: coder/code-server
Release: v4.122.0
Tag: v4.122.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2026-05-28T23:59:00Z; created_at: 2026-05-28T21:22:08Z
Release notes:
Code v1.122.0
### Changed
- Update to Code 1.122.0
### Fixed
- `--app-name` will now affect window titles within the editor (it is now used
as the value for `${appName}` in the title template) as well as some other
places like the help > about dialog.
### Added
- App name can now be set with the `CODE_SERVER_APP_NAME` environment variable.
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the coder/code-server release v4.122.0, the project updates to Code 1.122.0. The --app-name flag now affects window titles within the editor (used as the value for ${appName} in the title template) and other places like the help > about dialog. Additionally, the app name can now be set with the CODE_SERVER_APP_NAME environment variable.

questions.query_text: In the coder/code-server releases, which release adds the --cookie-suffix flag and the authorizationHeaderToken property?

answer_support.answer_text: adds a new --cookie-suffix flag ... supports a new authorizationHeaderToken property

questions.query_text: Across the coder/code-server releases, how does the change to --app-name in v4.122.0 differ from the change to --app-name in v4.111.0?

options.text: v4.111.0 makes --app-name affect the error page title, while v4.122.0 makes it affect window titles within the editor and adds the CODE_SERVER_APP_NAME environment variable.
v4.111.0 removes --app-name entirely, while v4.122.0 reintroduces it for window titles.
Both releases only update the underlying Code version without changing --app-name behavior.
v4.111.0 adds the CODE_SERVER_APP_NAME environment variable, while v4.122.0 makes --app-name affect the error page title.

questions.expected_answer: v4.111.0 makes --app-name affect the error page title, while v4.122.0 makes it affect window titles within the editor and adds the CODE_SERVER_APP_NAME environment variable.

answer_support.answer_text: --app-name now affects the error page title
--app-name will now affect window titles within the editor ... app name can now be set with the CODE_SERVER_APP_NAME environment variable

questions.query_text: In the coder/code-server release window, order the following release-content states from earliest to latest: (1) a release that adds the --reconnection-grace-time flag and wildcard trusted origins, (2) a release that fixes native modules to use the correct glibc version, (3) a release that re-packages correct Node binaries for arm64 and armv7l, (4) a release that updates to Code 1.122.0 and extends --app-name to window titles.

answer_support.answer_text: adds the --reconnection-grace-time flag ... Trusted origins can now begin with '*.'
re-release of v4.113.0 but with the correct Node binaries for arm64 and armv7l
Ensure native modules are built from source so they use the correct version of glibc
Update to Code 1.122.0 ... --app-name will now affect window titles within the editor
